mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference vs baseline is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across durable and non-durable demand channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals by >5% Sharpe ratio or quarterly return correlation drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when inflation exceeds 3% annually or during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation months or inflation threshold shows no statistical edge over a 2.0% cutoff.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening without statistical artifacts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression or signal accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and earnings, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices that manifests as positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions erase initial signal edge or 60-day return correlation with GDP growth falls below 0.10 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency during sharp production drops remains below 45% or signal is subsumed by broader market volatility factors.
